titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Herbert Thoms Photographs Used in the Publication Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut
Thoms (Herbert) Photographs Used in the Publication Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 10:43

did.unittitle: Herbert Thoms photographs used in the publication Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut
did.abstract: The materials consist of photographs and illustrations used by Herbert Thoms (Yale Medical 1910) in his book Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut (1967). Included are photographs of houses in New Haven and the surrounding areas of Yale historical figures. Most of the materials in Series I are negatives. Thoms's scrapbook in Series II is composed of descriptions and photographs of memorials and gravestones of men who played important roles in Yale's history.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 48-A (YRG 48-A), Yale pictorial records and collections.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Herbert Thoms Photographs Used in the Publication Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut (RU 656). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in two series: I. Illustrations, Photographs, and Negatives, circa 1967; II. Scrapbook of Memorials and Gravestones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of photographs and illustrations used by Herbert Thoms (Yale Medical 1910) in his book Yale Men and Landmarks in Old Connecticut (1967). Included are photographs of houses in New Haven and the surrounding areas of Yale historical figures. Most of the materials in Series I are negatives. Thoms's scrapbook in Series II is composed of descriptions and photographs of memorials and gravestones of men who played important roles in Yale's history.

did.unittitle: Eli Ives Memorial--Timothy Dwight College, New Haven
Gov. Luzon B. Morris House--Sandy Hook
Thomas Ruggles Jr. and Sr. Memorial--Guilford
